topic: Someone discovers their partner has been writing a novel about them
raw_output:
[story 1]

The first time Leo found the manuscript, he was halfway through making coffee. It was tucked beneath a stack of forgotten library books, a thick, cream-colored folder. Curiosity, a sensation he usually managed to keep leashed, tugged him toward it. When he finally opened it, the words swam before his eyes. Every sentence, every intimate detail, was undeniably about him. A slow, cold dread began to pool in his stomach. He stared at the page, his jaw tightening. He didn't move to close the file immediately. Instead, he picked up a pen, his hand trembling slightly, and traced a line of prose with his fingertip. "This is... elaborate," he murmured to the empty apartment, the sound thin and brittle. He felt a strange, stubborn refusal to accept the reality unfolding before him. He decided he would wait until tomorrow, when the shock might have worn off, and then he would confront the situation with a carefully constructed wall of silence.

[story 2]

"You haven't seen this, have you?" Sarah asked, her voice pitched unnervingly high, gesturing toward the laptop screen glowing on Mark’s desk. He was deeply engrossed, headphones clamped over his ears, a furrow permanently etched between his brows. When he finally pulled them off, a flush crept up his neck. The screen displayed chapters—intimate, detailed narratives where he was the protagonist. Sarah’s face was a mask of bewildered hurt. Mark merely leaned back in his chair, crossing his arms tightly over his chest, his posture rigid. "It's just a story," he said, the tone flat, devoid of warmth. He pushed a stray lock of hair back from his forehead, his gaze fixed on a spot just beyond her shoulder. He felt a deep, internal resistance to acknowledging the gravity of the situation. He needed space, needed time to process this unexpected intrusion, and he made a decision to ignore the sharp sting of her disappointment, focusing instead on the mundane task of organizing his desk, moving with a deliberate, almost stiff precision.

[story 3]

He found the evidence while tidying the shared storage closet. Tucked inside a box of old vinyl records was a bound journal, filled with florid descriptions of their shared moments, their inside jokes, their vulnerabilities. Reading the meticulous prose made his skin prickle with a strange mix of alarm and infuriating stubbornness. He wanted to slam the book shut, to deny the existence of these words, but his fingers lingered on the paper. He stood there for a long time, breathing shallowly, the silence of the closet amplifying the frantic thumping in his chest. When his partner walked in, concern softening the lines around their eyes, he managed a clipped, dismissive reply. "That's... a hobby," he stated, his voice carefully level, refusing to let his internal turmoil show. He deliberately avoided looking at the journal, focusing instead on the dust motes dancing in the single shaft of light cutting across the floor, determined to maintain a facade of complete indifference.
